content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcar PnacrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
January 6,2005
Mehdi Morshed, Executive Director
Califomia High-Speed Rail Authority
925 L Street, Suite 1425
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-04-273
Dear Mr. Morshed:
This leffer is in response to your request on behalf of the California High-Speed
Rail Authority (*AuthoritJ/') for advice regarding the giftprovisions of the political
Reform Act (the'Act"). I
QUESTTON
May proposed payments made by the Japanese-California High Speed Rail Study
Group ("Study Group') and the Japan Overseas Rolling Stock Association (.TORSA)to
host 15 individuals, including California state and locaipublic officials, be considered
gifts to the Authority and not to the individual public officials if the requirernents of
regulation 189M2 are met?
CONCLUSION
So long as the donors and the Authority comply with regulati on lB944.2,the
payments will not be "gifts" to public officials of the Authority. However, otherpublic
officials, those who accept the invitation for travel and are not on the staflor board of the
Authority, will be receiving a gift from the Authority which may be subject to reporting,
although not subject to limits.
FACTS
The Study Group, in conjunction with JORSA, has extended an invitation to a
maximum of 15 individuals to be selected by the Authority to experience the Shinkansen
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections


File No. A-04-273
Page No. 2
high speed train in Japan and to visit key facilities of the Shinkansen system. All costs
associated with transportation, lodging, and meals will be covered by the Study Group
and JORSA.
The Study Group has not extended invitations to specific individuals. Rather, the
invitation is extended to those individuals chosen by the Authority who are board
members of the Authority, state legislators, administrative representatives, local
governmental leaders, members of the business community, and Authority staff
members. The Authority is not required to make a selection from each category, but
rather the list of categories serves to illustrate the tlpes of individuals the Stuay Group
would like to see participate in the proposed trip.
Although the letter suggests the proposed fravel take place in the fall of 2004, any
such trip will not occur until the spring of 2005, at the earliest. The Authority intends to
select the participants at its January 2005 meeting.
ANALYSIS
Public officials, defined in substantive part in section 82048 as "every mernber,
officer, employee or consultant of a state or local govemment ageney," are piohibited
from receiving grfts ofmore than $360 from reportable sources on his orher statement of
economic interests. (Section 89503 and regulation 18940.2.) (Individuals who are not
public officials are not subject to the Act's restrictions.)
A grft is defined, in part, in section 82028 as:
"...any pa5tment that confers a personal benefit on the recipient, to the
extent that consideration ofequal or greater value is not received and
includes a rebate or discount in the price of anything of value unless the
rebate or discount is made in the regular course of business to mernbers of
the public without regard to official status.,'
However, regulation 18944.2 provides a mechanism by which a gift may be
deemed made to the agency and not to the officials of the agency. Undei the regulation,
all of the following requirements must be met in order for this exception to app[:
"(1) The agency receives and controls the payment.
Q) The payment is used for official agency business.
(3) The agency, in its sole discretion, determines the specific official or
officials who shall use the payment. Howevetr, the donor may identifr
a specific putpose for the agency's use of the payment, so long as the
donor does not designate the specific official or officials who may use
the payment.
(a) The agency memorializes the payment in a written pubric record
which embodies the requirements of subdivisions (aXr) to (a)(3) of
this regulation [18944.2] set forth above and which:


File No. A-04-273
Page No. 3
(A) Identifies the donor and the official, officials, or class of
officials receiving or using the payment;
(B) Describes the ofificial agency use and the nature and amount
of the payment; and
(c) Is filed with the agency official who maintains the records of
the agency's statements ofeconomic interests where the
agency has a specific office for the maintenance of such
statements, or where no specific office exists for the
maintenance of such statements, at a designated office of the
agency, and the filing is done within 30 days of the receipt of
the payment by the agqrcy. (Regulation 18944.2.)
Therefore, if the criteria in regulation 189M.2 are met, the Authority officials who
participate in the proposed travel would not be limited to $360, norwould theybe
required to report the payments on their statements of economic interests.
This exception will apply only to public officials of the Authority; otherpublic
officials who participate will be deemed to have received a gift from tne eu*rority, which
may be reportable on their statements of economic interests.2 All state and local elected
officers, and other individuals specified in section 872}O,are required to report gifts of
$50 ormore from the Authority.3 (Sections 87203 and87207.) Whetherm-embers of a
state board or commission or designated employees of a state or local govemment age11cy
are required to report gifts from the Authority depends on the conflict of interest code foi
their individual agencies.
While gifts of travel to these other public officials must be reported, it does not
appear that they will be subject to the $360 limit. (Section 89506 and regulation
I 8950. I .) Regulation I 8950. I O) provides that:
'A payment made for kavel, including actual transportation and related
lodging and subsistencg is not subject to the prohibitions or limitations on
honoraria and gifts specified in Government Code Sections 89501, Bg5A2,
or 89503 if:
(l) The fravel is reasonablyrelated to a legislativo or governmental
pu{pose, or to an issue of state, national, or internafional public policy
and
(2) The payment is provided by a government, a governmental agency, a
foreign government, a governmental authority ....
2 Because the Authority will be directing and controlling the gift of bavel by deciding which
public officials will be invited to participate, the Authority is considered the sowce oithe gift.-@egulation
tledta.2@)(t).)
3 While generally, the burden of co-pMng with the gift limits of the Act is applicable to the
public official, the authority (the donor) also has duties under the Act and is subject to iiability gnder
section 89521 for the making of a gift or expenditure in violation of the gift limits. Thus, we udrrir"
concerning the authority's duties in connection with public officials designated by the authority to receive
the gifts.


FileNo. A-04-273
Page No. 4
"Excqtt as provided by Califomia Code of Regulations, Title 2, Section
18950.3, any payment made for transportation, lodging, and subsistence,
specified by subdivision (b), shall be reported in accordance with
Government Code Section 87207 (c)."
It appears that the activities for which the travel is proposed are reasonably related to a
governmental purpose or to a state issue. Thereforg public officials invited by the
Authority to participate, who are not board or staff members to the Authority, must report
the value of the gift, even though the amount is not subject to the $360limit.a
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
n
By:
S. Moen, PhD
Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division
KSMjg
I:\Advicelfrs\04-273
u Please note that Article XII, Section 7, of the California Constitution, states rhat "[a]
transportation company may not grant free passes or discormts to anyone holding an office in this state; and
the acceptance of a pass or discount by a public ofEcer, other than a Fublic Utilities Commissioner, shall
work a forfeiture of that ofEce." Contact the Attorney General's office at (916) 324-5765 if you have any
questions concerning this prohibition.

Authority, will be receiving a gift from the Authority which may be subject to reporting,
although not subject to limits.
